name: Tuwo Shinkafa
intro: Made using either short grain rice or fine rice flour, this swallow is eaten in the northern parts of Nigeria and is incredibly easy to make.
method: Pour 2 cups of cold water into a pot, along with the salt, and add the rice flour. Stir quickly with a wooden spoon and keep mixing over medium-high heat until a sticky dough forms. Add 1 cup of hot water and do not stir. Cover and simmer for 5-6 minutes over low heat. Mix vigorously with a wooden spoon until you have a thick, smooth dough.

Add the wheat meal and 1 cup of cold water to a pot. Keep stirring with a wooden spoon over low-medium until it starts to thicken slightly. Add the half cup of water, cover and leave to simmer on low heat for 10 minutes. Mix and knead the wheat with a wooden spoon until you have a thick, smooth, dough-like consistency.
